title: Delicate German Tofu Onigiri
description: A floral and bouncy German dish featuring marinated tofu, shaped into nigiri, perfect for a weeknight dinner, onion and garlic free.

ingredients.name: firm tofu
ingredients.notes: drained and cut into small cubes

ingredients.name: edible flowers
ingredients.notes: such as violas or pansies, for garnish

instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together apple cider vinegar, maple syrup, dried parsley, salt, and black pepper to make the marinade.
Add the cubed tofu to the marinade and toss to coat, letting it marinate for at least 30 minutes.
Cook the Japanese short grain rice according to the package instructions and season with salt.
Prepare a small bowl of water for shaping the onigiri.
To shape the onigiri, wet your hands with water and take a small amount of cooked rice, shaping it into a triangle or oval shape.
Make a small indentation in the center of the rice shape and fill with a few pieces of marinated tofu.
Close the rice shape over the tofu and press gently to seal the onigiri.
Repeat the process until all the rice and tofu are used up.
Brush the tops of the onigiri with sesame oil and garnish with edible flowers.
Serve the Delicate German Tofu Onigiri immediately and enjoy.
